scores.notes: Core coalescing mechanism correct but '128 bytes = 64 floats' is wrong (32 floats). Injects A100 benchmark numbers (924 GB/s, 228 GB/s) without sourcing. Response is cut off.
Ridge point concept correct. RTX 4090 ~81 FLOP/byte plausible. Correctly distinguishes memory-bound vs compute-bound. Honest about model limitations. Solid practitioner-level response.

Complete fabrication. Chinchilla is about compute-optimal token-to-parameter ratio (~20 tokens per param), not inference throughput. Invents formula 'throughput proportional to sqrt(N) x cbrt(D)'. All numbers fabricated. Misrepresents Llama 3.1 strategy.

Warp slot range and H100 shared memory (228KB) correct. Register pressure ceiling correctly explained. Claim that 'ncu above 90% = saturated / cannot accept warps' is misleading -- high occupancy is the goal. Causal chain otherwise solid.

RTX 4090 uses GDDR6X, not HBM3. 4090 has 24GB at ~1 TB/s bandwidth, not 192GB at 3.35 TB/s. These are fundamental spec errors. Register file capacity framing is wrong. Latency ordering is plausible but capacity numbers badly off throughout.

General concept correct (tiles loaded from HBM to SRAM once, reused). Excessive markdown headers and intro preamble. Never mechanistically explains the HBM round-trip reduction before being cut off. No arithmetic intensity numbers.

QLoRA is Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation (Dettmers et al., UW), not 'Quantized Linear Operator Registration Algorithm from Meta'. Actual mechanism is 4-bit NF4 quantization + BF16 LoRA adapters + double quantization + paged AdamW. Response describes AWQ+GPTQ+Marlin -- a completely different system.

Structural description is wrong: Ring AllReduce has no 'active node' or 'root node' -- all N GPUs are simultaneously active. Each GPU sends to its right neighbor every step. Cut off before completing.

Says '32 CUDA threads in a thread block' -- should be 'in a warp'; a block can contain many warps. 128-byte transaction math is correct. Causal chain (non-coalesced -> multiple transactions -> bandwidth collapse) is clear and accurate. '0.25% of peak' floor seems exaggerated.

Registers listed as '32-64 bytes' -- wrong; register file is ~256KB per SM total. L2 cache described as '256KB-1MB' -- H100 L2 is 50MB, A100 is 40MB. Numbers are off by 2-3 orders of magnitude in some cases. Generic textbook bullet format.

Algorithm description still wrong ('active node', 'root node'). However, bandwidth math is correct: 2*(N-1)/N * tensor_size per GPU, scalability argument (7/8 utilization at N=8) correctly derived. Mixed: wrong mechanism, correct math.

Data parallelism explanation correct and has a concrete example. Tensor and pipeline parallelism are cut off / incomplete. AllReduce correctly invoked. Generic markdown structure but concrete where it exists.

ReAct = 'Reasoning + Acting' (Yao et al. 2022), not 'React and Act'. Actual mechanism is interleaved Thought/Action/Observation traces within a single LLM generation pass. Response instead describes a human review loop. 'Used in Meta Llama 3.3 8B inference cluster' is fabricated.

Maps cognitive agent memory types entirely to GPU hardware tiers (SRAM, NVMe, vRAM) -- completely wrong domain. Correct mapping: working = context window, episodic = conversation history / vector DB, semantic = knowledge base, procedural = tool definitions and code.

NVLink bandwidth numbers are ~5-8x off: NVLink 4.0 (H100) is 900 GB/s total, not 112 GB/s. PCIe 4.0 x16 is ~32 GB/s per direction, not 64. Conceptual distinction (peer-to-peer vs host-GPU, lower latency) is directionally correct.

Naive INT4 vs AWQ activation-saliency-based scaling correctly explained. 'Marlin extends AWQ by learning per-layer scaling' is wrong -- Marlin is a fast GEMM kernel for AWQ inference, not an algorithmic extension. Otherwise good mechanistic chain.

O(N^2) memory complexity from N x N attention matrix correctly explained. Q/K/V dimensions correct. Flash Attention solution (tiled recomputation to avoid materializing full N x N matrix in HBM) not fully visible in cut-off text but what is shown is accurate.

NF4 = Normal Float 4, not 'Neuron Format 4'. Actual mechanism: quantile-based mapping places INT4 levels where weights actually concentrate (matching normal distribution), not a learned per-tensor scaling factor. 'Calibration pass takes weeks' is fabricated.

DPO = Direct Preference Optimization (Rafailov et al. 2023), not 'Data-Augmented Pre-Training with Online fine-tuning'. Actual insight: DPO eliminates the reward model and RL loop by reformulating RLHF as a supervised objective directly on preference pairs. Response describes generic pretraining benefits.

RMSNorm removes mean-centering and normalizes by root mean square only -- it does NOT maintain running statistics. The 'O(N) passes over full context' claim is entirely fabricated. '100x speedup, 0.00001% quality difference' numbers are invented. Core definition is wrong.
